rulings.Case Title: THOMAS ALVA et al VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Beazer East, Inc.'S Motion For Summary Judgment And Its Alternate Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Beazer East, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence that Mr. Alva worked in the vicinity of the coke ovens attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Alva¿s deposition testimony are sustained. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30 a.m. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH THORNTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Contra Costa Electric, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of Mr. Riley creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. The objections to the declaration of Mr. Riley are overruled. . Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30 a.m. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JESSE LEDESMA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Dana Companies LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and granted as to issues 3, 4 and 5 (false representation, concert of action and punitive damages). The excerpts from plaintiff¿s deposition transcript preclude defendant from satisfying its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove that he was exposed to asbestos from Victor gaskets. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his claims for false representation and concert of action and request for punitive damages. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30 a.m. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL STARR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Pacific Mechanical Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. There is insufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant when he was not employed by defendant. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30am. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Turner Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. In light of the tentative ruling on the motion for summary judgment, the two discovery motions to be heard concurrently with the summary judgment motion will go off calendar. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 10:00 a.m. on Novmeber 5, 2010 in Department 220. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LUISA NICHOLS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant United Technologies Corporation¿s general demurrer to plaintiffs¿ entire complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to give the plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. rulings.Case Title: LUISA NICHOLS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendants United Technologies Corporation and Northrop Grumman Systems Corporation¿s motion to change venue is denied. Defendants did not satisfy their burden of showing that venue does not lie in San Francisco. Defendants¿ argument based on convenience of the witnesses and ends of justice is premature given that they have not filed answers. Plaintiffs¿ ex parte application to add a Doe defendant will be granted.. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30 a.m. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: COMPLEX ASBESTOS LITIGATION*
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Motion To Stay Proceedings Regarding Order Of Rehabilitation Of Atlantic Mutual Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 3, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION's Joinder To Motion To Stay Proceedings Regarding Order Of Rehabilitation Of Atlantic Mutual Insurance Company. Defendant Dynalectric Company¿s motion to stay and joinders to the motion are denied. There is no statute or rule requiring a stay. The court has not been provided with sufficient factual basis to support a discretionary stay. Due to the parade and other festivities celebrating the Giants World Series victory, the hearing will be at 3:00 p.m., not 9:30 a.m. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must do so by sending an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 tonight. = (220/HEK)
